entry.subjective: 55yo F with T2DM x 4y reports polyuria and fatigue over the past month. Adherent to metformin 1000mg BID, denies hypoglycemia, no dietitian visits in the last year.
entry.objective: HR 84 bpm, BP 132/82, BMI 31. HbA1c 8.6% (2024-03-12), trending up from 7.9% one year prior. No peripheral neuropathy on monofilament exam.
entry.assessment: Type 2 diabetes, uncontrolled. CMS122 denominator: yes, numerator (HbA1c > 9.0%): not yet but trending. Risk for poor control.
entry.plan: Add empagliflozin 10mg daily. Reinforce carb counting. Re-check HbA1c in 3 months. Referral to RD. Next visit Sep 2024.
entry.author: dr.kessler@example.com

entry.subjective: Patient verbalizes understanding of new SGLT2 inhibitor. Concerned about cost.
entry.objective: Vitals stable. Glucometer log reviewed, fasting glucose 140-180 mg/dL most mornings. Demonstrated correct injection technique not applicable (oral regimen).
entry.assessment: Knowledge deficit re: SGLT2 side effects (UTI risk, euglycemic DKA signs).
entry.plan: Provided written teaching sheet. Reviewed sick day rules. Scheduled f/u nurse call in 2 weeks. Coordinating with case worker re: medication cost.
entry.author: rn.morales@example.com

entry.subjective: Returns at 6 months. Reports better energy but missed several days of empagliflozin due to cost. Diet adherence partial.
entry.objective: BP 130/80. HbA1c 9.4% (2024-09-04), up from 8.6%. No signs of retinopathy on prior dilated exam.
entry.assessment: T2DM with worsening control. Now exceeding poor-control threshold. CMS122 numerator: yes.
entry.plan: Switch SGLT2 to formulary alternative. Add GLP-1 RA pending PA. Continue metformin. Repeat HbA1c in 3 months. Endocrine referral.
entry.author: dr.kessler@example.com

entry.subjective: Patient reports $40 copay for empagliflozin is unaffordable on fixed income.
entry.objective: Reviewed insurance formulary. Confirmed dapagliflozin and metformin are Tier 1.
entry.assessment: Social determinant: medication affordability driving non-adherence.
entry.plan: Submitted manufacturer copay-assistance application. Coordinated with PCP to switch to formulary alternative. F/u call in 1 week to confirm fill.
entry.author: cw.patel@example.com

entry.subjective: Endocrine f/u. Patient on max metformin + dapagliflozin + semaglutide 0.5mg weekly x 8 weeks. Tolerating well, 6 lb wt loss.
entry.assessment: T2DM, persistent uncontrolled despite triple therapy. Suspect non-adherence vs disease progression.
entry.plan: Increase semaglutide to 1.0mg weekly. CGM trial. Discussed insulin if HbA1c remains > 9.0% next visit. Repeat A1c in 4 months.
entry.author: dr.singh@example.com

entry.subjective: CGM data shows fasting glucose 160-220, post-prandial spikes to 280. Reports skipping semaglutide due to nausea x 3 doses.
entry.objective: BP 130/82. HbA1c 10.2% (2025-07-15). Wt unchanged. No DKA.
entry.assessment: T2DM, poorly controlled (CMS122 numerator: yes). Plan failure on oral + GLP-1.
entry.plan: Start basal insulin glargine 10 units qHS, titrate to fasting glucose < 130. Continue metformin and dapagliflozin. Resume semaglutide at 0.25mg with anti-emetic. RN to call in 1 week.
entry.author: dr.singh@example.com

entry.subjective: 57yo M, longstanding essential HTN x 5y. Reports occasional morning headaches. Adherent to lisinopril 20mg daily. Salt intake moderate, exercises 2x/wk.
entry.objective: BP 148/94 (2024-02-09), repeat 146/92. HR 78. BMI 28. No signs of end-organ damage. EKG NSR.
entry.assessment: Stage 2 hypertension, uncontrolled per JNC8. CMS165 denominator: yes, numerator (BP < 140/90): no.
entry.plan: Add amlodipine 5mg daily. Counseled DASH diet. Home BP log requested. Recheck in 6 months.
entry.author: dr.kessler@example.com

entry.subjective: Patient open to lifestyle counseling. Wife present and supportive.
entry.objective: Home BP cuff validated against office cuff (within 4 mmHg). Reviewed proper technique.
entry.plan: Provided BP log template. F/u nurse call in 1 month to review readings.
entry.author: rn.morales@example.com

entry.subjective: 6-month f/u. Tolerating amlodipine, mild ankle edema, no orthostasis. Home BP avg 142/88.
entry.assessment: HTN, improved but still above goal. CMS165 numerator: no.
entry.plan: Increase amlodipine to 10mg. Continue lisinopril 20mg. Recheck in 6 months.
entry.author: dr.kessler@example.com

entry.subjective: Annual visit. Reports good adherence, exercising 4x/wk. Home BP avg 132/82. No edema since dose change.
entry.objective: BP 134/84 (2025-05-02). BMI 27.4. Basic metabolic panel normal.
entry.assessment: HTN, controlled. CMS165 numerator: yes (BP < 140/90 on latest reading).
entry.plan: Continue current regimen. Annual labs ordered. Recheck in 12 months unless symptoms.
entry.author: dr.kessler@example.com

entry.subjective: Patient proud of weight loss and BP control. Asks about reducing meds.
entry.objective: BP log reviewed, consistent with office reading.
entry.assessment: On track. Reinforce that dose reduction requires sustained control x 1y per provider.
entry.plan: Encouraged continued lifestyle. Scheduled labs draw same day.
entry.author: rn.morales@example.com

entry.subjective: 30yo G2P1 at 28 weeks. Reports mild swelling in hands and feet, denies headache, no visual changes, no RUQ pain. Fetal movement reassuring.
entry.objective: BP 134/86, urine dipstick trace protein. Fundal height appropriate. FHTs 148 bpm.
entry.assessment: Pregnancy 28 wks. Borderline BP, mild edema. Watch for pre-eclampsia.
entry.plan: Order 24h urine protein and CMP. Re-check BP in 1 wk. Pre-eclampsia precaution handout given.
entry.author: dr.nguyen@example.com

entry.subjective: Returns at 37 wks. Reports new-onset right-sided headache yesterday, resolved with rest. No visual changes. Fetal movement normal.
entry.objective: BP 152/96, repeat 150/98. Urine protein 2+. AST 38, ALT 42, platelets 165k. Hyperreflexive at patella.
entry.assessment: Pre-eclampsia with severe features developing. Admit for fetal monitoring and BP control.
entry.plan: Admit L&D. Start labetalol IV. Magnesium sulfate for seizure prophylaxis. Plan delivery within 24-48h. Consult MFM.
entry.author: dr.nguyen@example.com

entry.subjective: POD#1 from cesarean for non-reassuring fetal status in setting of severe pre-eclampsia. EBL 1800 mL intra-op. Received 2 units pRBC. Reports incisional pain 5/10, controlled with PCA. Voiding via foley.
entry.objective: BP 162/108 on labetalol drip. HR 92. Hgb 8.4 (post-transfusion). Lochia moderate. Fundus firm at U. Lungs clear.
entry.assessment: Severe pre-eclampsia with postpartum hemorrhage. Severe maternal morbidity (ePC02 numerator: yes - delivery encounter with transfusion). Anemia.
entry.plan: Continue magnesium x 24h post-delivery. Wean labetalol to PO when stable. Iron supplementation. Strict I/O. Pneumatic compression. F/u Hgb in 12h.
entry.author: dr.nguyen@example.com

entry.subjective: Patient teary, asking when she can hold the baby. Pain controlled at rest.
entry.objective: Continuous BP monitoring q15min. Magnesium drip at 2g/h. Reflexes 2+. UO > 30 mL/h. Lochia changed q2h.
entry.assessment: Stable post-op. Bonding interrupted by ICU-level monitoring.
entry.plan: Coordinated with NICU to bring baby to bedside. Reviewed mag side-effects with patient. F/u BP and reflexes hourly.
entry.author: rn.morales@example.com

entry.subjective: 3-week postpartum visit. Breastfeeding well. Denies headache or visual changes. Mood baseline. No chest pain or SOB.
entry.objective: BP 138/88 on PO labetalol 200mg BID. Incision well-healed. Hgb 10.6.
entry.assessment: Postpartum recovery from severe pre-eclampsia and PPH. BP improving. Anemia resolving.
entry.plan: Continue labetalol, plan to taper at 6 wk visit if BP < 130/80. Iron PO. EPDS screen negative. Discussed contraception. Counseled on future-pregnancy risk and value of low-dose aspirin in any subsequent pregnancy.
entry.author: dr.nguyen@example.com
